# First signed release — required identity values

The updater/install flow is fully wired (frontend `src/lib/updater.ts` +
`UpdateBanner`, `updater:default` capability, NSIS passive install mode). Before
the **first signed Windows release** these placeholder values in
`src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` must be replaced with real ones — the
`Assert updater release config is real` step in
[`release.yml`](./workflows/release.yml) fails the build until they are:

- [ ] **`identifier`** — replace `com.example.fileexplorer` with the real
      reverse-DNS bundle id. The updater/signing identity is keyed off this.
- [ ] **`plugins.updater.pubkey`** — replace
      `REPLACE_WITH_TAURI_UPDATER_PUBLIC_KEY_BEFORE_SIGNED_RELEASE` with the
      public key generated by `pnpm tauri signer generate`.
- [ ] **`plugins.updater.endpoints[0]`** — replace the `OWNER/REPO` path with the
      canonical `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/releases/latest/download/latest.json`.

CI secrets required for signing: `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY` and
`TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD`.

> macOS ships **unsigned** in v1 (decision D15) — no Apple identity needed yet.
> For an explicit cross-platform relaunch after update, add
> `@tauri-apps/plugin-process` + the `process:default` capability and call
> `relaunch()` in `downloadAndInstallAppUpdate` (see the TODO there).
